title: Read Device Health From the Catalyst Center Intent API

problem.statement: Cisco Catalyst Center (formerly DNA Center) exposes a public Intent API. A GET to /dna/intent/api/v1/device-health returns the JSON payload shown below, wrapping results in a top-level 'response' list (this is the standard Catalyst Center Intent API envelope — nearly every endpoint returns {"response": [...], "version": "..."}). Which expression correctly reads the CPU utilization of the first device in the response?
problem.use_case: Catalyst Center centralizes health data for an entire Cisco fleet behind one Intent API, so automation scripts poll this single endpoint instead of SSH-ing into every device individually — but nearly every response is wrapped in this same {"response": [...]} envelope, and forgetting to index into the list (not just the dict) is the most common parsing mistake against this API.

hints: Catalyst Center's Intent API always wraps results in a top-level 'response' key, and for multi-device endpoints that value is a list, not a single dict.
You need to index into the list before reading device fields — response.json()['response'] gives you a list of devices, not one device.

solution.explanation: Catalyst Center's Intent API wraps results in a top-level 'response' list, so you must index into element [0] (the first device) before reading its 'cpuUtilization' field: response.json()['response'][0]['cpuUtilization'].
